input.title: Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 accelerates healing of rat Achilles tendon transection
input.abstract: We evaluated BPC 157 effect on Achilles tendon-to-bone healing in rats. Forty-eight male Sprague-Dawley rats underwent complete Achilles tendon transection and were randomized to BPC 157 (10 µg/kg intraperitoneally daily, n=24) or saline (n=24) for 14 days. Biomechanical testing on day 14 showed maximal load to failure increased 56% in the BPC 157 group vs saline (32.1 ± 4.8 vs 20.6 ± 3.2 N, p<0.001). Histologic analysis revealed denser collagen fiber organization. No adverse events or mortality were observed. We conclude BPC 157 accelerates functional tendon-to-bone healing in this model.
